summary: Taxonomy-mapping review accepts the candidate. The no-op review-landscape records stay scoped to the senolytics track and cellular-senescence hallmark, use research-session/search-log/screening-run records for workflow evidence, and leave the coverage-assessment review/meta-analysis category marked thin rather than adding a new evidence source.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed research session, search log, and screening run are scoped to senolytics and cellular senescence and do not introduce intervention-specific or unrelated hallmark mappings.
findings.why_it_matters: The repair should route future work to the right track without expanding the evidence map beyond the failed search scope.
findings.recommended_action: No taxonomy revision required; keep the no-op workflow records under senolytics/cellular_senescence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage update keeps the unresolved item in the recent_reviews_meta_analyses/review-landscape gap rather than classifying it as a screened source, clinical result, or synthesis-ready review.
findings.why_it_matters: Accurate category mapping prevents downstream consumers and triage agents from treating a blocked no-op search as included review evidence.
findings.recommended_action: Accept this lane and require a later search/screening candidate to add concrete review source, eligibility, and source-snapshot records before changing the coverage category.
